title: Elatostema brachyodontum
taxonomy: Elatostema brachyodontum is a species of plant in the family Urticaceae, commonly known as the nettle family. The Urticaceae family is a large group of flowering plants that are often found in tropical and subtropical regions. Elatostema brachyodontum is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Elatostema, which consists of around 400 species. The plant's classification also places it within the order Urticales and the clade rosids. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Elatostema brachyodontum and its related species.

intro: Elatostema brachyodontum, commonly known as the Himalayan polynome, is a species of flowering plant in the nettle family Urticaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Elatostema brachyodontum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
